result.summary: storage.googleapis.com exposes Cloud Storage HTTP APIs (JSON and XML), a Cloud Storage MCP surface, and Google Cloud CLIs (`gcloud storage`, `gsutil`); OAuth 2.0 Google Cloud credentials are documented for the JSON API and CLIs, while XML API and MCP auth details were not fully pinned from the docs read. Public unauthenticated access is also supported for objects that bucket owners make public.
result.description: Google Cloud Storage is Google's managed object storage service for storing and retrieving unstructured data in buckets and objects. It is used for application storage, archives, media delivery, analytics datasets, and similar object-storage workloads.

gcp_oauth_access_token.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token / Application Default Credentials
gcp_oauth_access_token.setup: Create a Google Cloud project and authenticate with Google Cloud. The easiest path for developers is to install the Google Cloud CLI and run `gcloud auth login` for user credentials or `gcloud auth application-default login` for ADC; service-to-service integrations can use a service account from the [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/). Cloud Storage docs describe using `Authorization: Bearer $(gcloud auth print-access-token)` for API calls; see [Cloud Storage authentication](https://docs.cloud.google.com/storage/docs/authentication).
